title: Observation Spaces
concept: How agents see the world: text logs, DOM trees, visual frames, or structured JSON payloads.

exercises.prompt: Last lesson: which of these counts as an 'environment' an AI agent can act in?

options.text: A web browser window with buttons and links
A printed paper book on a shelf

exercises.explain: An environment is the digital world the agent interacts with, like browsers, APIs, databases, or the operating system. A browser window is a classic example the agent can click and type in.

cards.title: Agents Need Eyes
cards.body: Before an agent can act, it has to sense what's happening. The way it 'sees' the world is called its observation space.

cards.title: What Is an Observation Space?
cards.body: An observation space is the form of information an agent receives about its environment. It can be text, a webpage's structure, a screenshot, or organized data like JSON.

cards.title: Like a Car Dashboard
cards.body: A driver senses the road through mirrors, gauges, and the windshield. An agent senses its world through logs, screenshots, or data feeds. Different windows into the same reality.

cards.title: Four Ways to See
cards.body: A text log shows lines of what happened. A DOM tree shows a webpage's structure. A visual frame is a screenshot. A JSON payload is neatly labeled data.

cards.title: Myth: Agents 'See' Like Humans
cards.body: Agents don't have real eyes. They receive data in whatever format is provided. A screenshot is just pixels; JSON is just labeled text. It all becomes numbers to process.

cards.title: Two Views of a Button
cards.body: The same 'Buy Now' button can be seen two very different ways depending on the observation space.

left.text: A screenshot showing a green button with the words 'Buy Now'.

right.text: Structured code: a button element labeled 'Buy Now' at a certain spot.

exercises.prompt: An agent receives neatly labeled data like {"temperature": 72, "city": "Miami"}. What kind of observation space is this?
exercises.explain: JSON is data organized into clear labels and values, like 'temperature' equals 72. This structured payload is one of the tidiest ways an agent can observe its world.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A screenshot given to an agent is called a visual frame.
exercises.explain: A visual frame is exactly that: an image or screenshot of what's on screen, made of pixels the agent must interpret.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank about how agents perceive webpages.
exercises.sentence: A DOM tree shows an agent the structure of a webpage instead of just a picture.
exercises.explain: A DOM tree describes the structure of a webpage, its buttons, links, and text as organized elements, rather than a flat image.

exercises.prompt: Which description of an observation space was written by an AI?

options.text: An observation space is the format in which an agent receives information about its environment, such as text logs, DOM trees, visual frames, or structured JSON payloads.
idk its like when the robot looks at stuff i guess??
Observation space = the eyeballs bolted onto the front of every AI agent.

exercises.explain: The first option is clear, complete, and correctly names all four observation types, the polished, structured style typical of AI writing. The others are vague or factually wrong.

exercises.prompt: An agent must click a 'Submit' button on a website but only receives a screenshot, no code. It struggles to find the exact button. What does this reveal about observation spaces?

options.text: The choice of observation space shapes what an agent can easily understand and do; a DOM tree might make the button easier to locate than raw pixels.
Screenshots are always the best observation space for every task.
Observation spaces don't affect an agent's abilities at all.
The agent has physical eyes that got blurry.

exercises.explain: How an agent sees the world directly affects what it can do. A screenshot (visual frame) is just pixels, so finding a button means guessing locations. A DOM tree gives labeled structure, making the button easy to find. Choosing the right observation space, text logs, DOM trees, visual frames, or JSON, is a key part of designing a capable agent.
